assessment.screening_summary: Audio-only clip with limited speech content and no visual cues. Patient appears to describe a picture scene but speech is unintelligible and transcript unavailable. No clear impairment-like signals observed. Further observation or multimodal assessment recommended.
evidence_against_risk: No observable speech errors, repetitions, or disorganization in available audio
No visible signs of distress, apathy, or emotional dysregulation
Task context (cookie theft picture description) is intact but unverifiable due to audio quality
alternative_explanations: Audio quality issues may mask subtle cognitive signals
Patient may be non-native speaker or experiencing temporary speech difficulty unrelated to dementia
Lack of visual data prevents assessment of gaze, facial expression, or motor behavior
risk_control_suggestions: Repeat screening with improved audio capture or visual recording
Use structured cognitive screening tools (e.g., MoCA, MMSE) in follow-up
Include caregiver report or functional history to supplement audio-only assessment
assessment.visit_recommendation: Formal cognitive screening in next visit with multimodal assessment
assessment.future_risk_trend_summary: Limited predictive value from single audio-only clip. Longitudinal assessment required for meaningful risk stratification.
context_notes: Video portion unavailable; assessment based solely on audio with low intelligibility
Patient role inferred from interaction context; examiner speech not evaluated
Task: cookie theft picture description; no verifiable performance due to audio quality
assessment.disclaimer: Research demo only. This output is not a diagnosis and must not be used as a standalone medical decision.
